headline: Oil’s Worst Case Scenario Is Here. $100 Crude Could Be Coming.
summary: The war in Iran has led to a worst-case scenario for the  oil  market: the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world’s oil flows.  On Saturday, about 4 million barrels of crude made it through—virtually all of Iranian origin, according to JP Morgan strategist Natasha Kaneva.  Wood Mackenzie analyst Alan Gelder thinks it’s plausible that oil flows are disrupted for weeks.

content.title: Oil’s Worst Case Scenario Is Here. $100 Crude Could Be Coming.
content.summary: The war in Iran has led to a worst-case scenario for the  oil  market: the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world’s oil flows.  On Saturday, about 4 million barrels of crude made it through—virtually all of Iranian origin, according to JP Morgan strategist Natasha Kaneva.  Wood Mackenzie analyst Alan Gelder thinks it’s plausible that oil flows are disrupted for weeks.

headline: Exxon Soars. Why Oil Stocks Could Go Even Higher.
summary: Exxon Mobil and other oil stocks were soaring early Monday amid a crude price surge linked to tit-for-tat attacks between the U.S., Israel and Iran.  Shares in the major oil companies were rallying in premarket trading.  Exxon  was up 4.6% to $159.49, while  Chevron  was trading 3.9% higher at $194.05.

headline: Energy stocks rise on oil prices, BlackRock consortium to acquire AES
summary: Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman tracks several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including energy stocks moving higher on spiking oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F), shares of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) getting a boost amid escalations in the Middle East after the US bombing of Iran over the weekend, and BlackRock-owned (BLK) Global Infrastructure Partners leading a consortium to acquire AES (AES) for $10.7 billion. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: The Worst Case Scenario for Oil Is Here. $100 Crude Could Be Coming.
summary: The war in Iran has led to a worst-case scenario for the  oil  market: the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world’s oil flows.  A prolonged closure could send international oil prices above $100 a barrel, a level they haven’t hit since 2022.  On Saturday, about 4 million barrels of crude made it through—virtually all of Iranian origin, according to JP Morgan strategist Natasha Kaneva.

content.title: The Worst Case Scenario for Oil Is Here. $100 Crude Could Be Coming.
content.summary: The war in Iran has led to a worst-case scenario for the  oil  market: the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20% of the world’s oil flows.  A prolonged closure could send international oil prices above $100 a barrel, a level they haven’t hit since 2022.  On Saturday, about 4 million barrels of crude made it through—virtually all of Iranian origin, according to JP Morgan strategist Natasha Kaneva.

headline: Why Berkshire Hathaway’s Earnings Weren’t as Bad as They Looked
summary: Berkshire Hathaway’s  fourth-quarter operating earnings weren’t great, but they were better than the headline decline of 30%.  Adjust for a $1.56 billion noncash goodwill impairment loss related to four of Berkshire’s subsidiaries, and some other one-time factors, and the drop in operating profits would have been closer to 20%, Barron’s estimates.  Berkshire included that after-tax impairment in its “Other” Income segment of operating income even as it excluded a $4.5 billion noncash write-down of its equity stake in  Occidental Petroleum  from its fourth-quarter operating income.

content.title: Why Berkshire Hathaway’s Earnings Weren’t as Bad as They Looked
content.summary: Berkshire Hathaway’s  fourth-quarter operating earnings weren’t great, but they were better than the headline decline of 30%.  Adjust for a $1.56 billion noncash goodwill impairment loss related to four of Berkshire’s subsidiaries, and some other one-time factors, and the drop in operating profits would have been closer to 20%, Barron’s estimates.  Berkshire included that after-tax impairment in its “Other” Income segment of operating income even as it excluded a $4.5 billion noncash write-down of its equity stake in  Occidental Petroleum  from its fourth-quarter operating income.

headline: Gas prices will soar 'very quickly' as Iran conflict boosts oil
summary: Oil (CL=F, BZ=F) prices are surging as the US's conflict with Iran escalates. GasBuddy head of petroleum analysis Patrick De Haan and Yahoo Finance Breaking Business News Reporter Jake Conley join Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman to discuss what higher oil prices mean for gas prices and consumers. headline: Oil Stocks Heat Up Amid Iran War As S&P 500 Slips; How High Will Oil Prices Go?
summary: Oil stocks crowded the top of the S&P 500 early Monday as the U.S.-Israel war against Iran entered its third day.  The war is damaging Iran's oil export infrastructure and snarling tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.  Supply worry boosted the U.S. crude oil price 6.4% to $71.30 a barrel in the futures market.

headline: Occidental Balances Debt Buybacks Carbon Capture Plans And Berkshire’s Growing Stake
summary: Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) launched cash tender offers to repurchase portions of its outstanding senior notes and debentures. The company is drawing increased attention for its carbon capture and storage plans alongside its traditional oil and gas operations. Berkshire Hathaway has been building an already large position in Occidental, leading to a more concentrated shareholder base. For you as an investor, Occidental sits at the intersection of conventional hydrocarbons and emerging...
